4. Multi-Year Equity Trends

Companies with 3+ years of proxy data, showing sustained expansion or contraction in outstanding equity positions. "Expanding" = unvested share counts growing across most filing periods. "Contracting" = shrinking, often indicating executives vesting/exercising faster than new grants are issued.

6. Equity Burn Rate Analysis

Burn rate measures how quickly companies are adding new equity to the outstanding pool vs. how quickly existing equity vests. A positive net burn rate means total outstanding equity (unvested shares + options) is growing — the company is granting faster than it’s vesting. A negative rate means the pool is shrinking.

Dataset-Wide Burn Rate by Year

Net change in total outstanding equity across all companies with consecutive-year data.

YearCompanies ExpandingStableContracting Net Shares Added Avg per Company
2019 339 94 109 136 -405.01M -1.19M
2020 348 104 105 139 +615.90M +1.77M
2021 365 92 107 166 +42.59M +117K
2022 375 122 108 145 +504.51M +1.35M
2023 379 107 128 144 -1,352.79M -3.57M
2024 370 100 125 145 +32.33M +87K
2025 369 110 124 135 -42.15M -114K
2026 19 1 8 10 +9.78M +515K

Average Burn Rate by Company

Average annual change in total outstanding equity (unvested shares + options) expressed as a percentage of the prior year’s outstanding equity pool. Positive = equity pool growing (granting > vesting); negative = shrinking (vesting > granting). Click a row to see year-by-year detail.
479 companies with multi-year data — 237 expanding, 240 contracting.

7. Underwater Options

Option grants where the exercise price exceeds the current stock price, meaning the options have no intrinsic value. Executives hold these positions but cannot profitably exercise them. Based on most recent filing per company vs. current market prices.

92 companies have underwater option positions across 926 individual grants, representing 60.91M shares with aggregate unrealized loss of $2.0B.

8. Key Patterns & Observations

  • Equity concentration is extreme: The top 10 companies by unvested equity value account for 100.0% of total unvested value ($586.6M of $586.6M). The top 50 companies likely represent the vast majority of all outstanding executive equity.
  • Instrument mix is shifting: 291 companies still have outstanding options, 468 have RSUs, and 388 use performance-based awards. 262 companies use both options and RSUs, reflecting the ongoing transition away from stock options.
  • Multi-year trends diverge: 54 companies show expanding equity overhang (more new grants than vesting), while 52 show contracting positions (vesting outpacing new grants). 332 show mixed patterns.
  • Year-over-year volatility is common: 4 year-over-year changes exceeded 100% in either direction. Large swings typically correspond to mega-grants (new CEO hires, periodic retention grants) or major executive departures (forfeiture of unvested equity).
  • Underwater options represent dead weight: 926 individual option positions are underwater. These options consume dilution capacity without creating executive alignment — the exercise price is above the current stock price, so they provide no economic incentive.
